response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced full-stack and systems software developer with strong craftsmanship credentials (TDD, low-latency DLang services, C/C++ tooling at National Instruments), and easily exceeds the years-of-experience bar with a BS in Computer Science. However, the role is a specialized hardware performance architecture position, and the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design — the core required skills. His performance experience is at the web-services level, not the silicon/microarchitecture level this role demands.
strengths: Extensive low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Long, diverse engineering career spanning embedded, desktop, and web with strong testing discipline
C/C++ background building engineering tooling (NI LabWindows/CVI)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory centers on full-stack product work, misaligned with principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and accelerator experience
Strong low-latency systems and performance optimization skills
